title.en: CRIMPING OR CURLING FIBRES, FILAMENTS, THREADS, OR YARNS; YARNS OR THREADS

Producing crimped or curled fibres, filaments, yarns, or threads, giving them latent characteristics (yarns per se D02G3/00; during formation of artificial filaments, threads, or the like D01D5/22; general aspects of chemical treatment D06M)
{by knitting, weaving or tufting, fixing and then unravelling}
{by heating fibres, filaments, yarns or threads so as to create a temperature gradient across their diameter, thereby imparting them latent asymmetrical shrinkage properties}
{by impinging the yarn against an uneven surface and thereby deforming it}
{with provision for imparting irregular effects to the yarn}
by twisting, fixing the twist and backtwisting, i.e. by imparting false twist
using knife edges, e.g. heated knife edges, for edge crimping
using grooved rollers or gear-wheel-type members
using jets or streams of turbulent gases, e.g. air, steam {(interlacing filaments D02J1/08)}
by combining fibres, filaments, or yarns, having different shrinkage characteristics {(D02G1/004 takes precedence)}
Combinations of two or more of the above-mentioned operations or devices; After-treatments for fixing crimp or curl
Yarns or threads, e.g. fancy yarns; Processes or apparatus for the production thereof, not otherwise provided for (for producing crimped or curled yarns D02G1/00)
Yarns or threads characterised by the material or by the materials from which they are made
Yarns or threads characterised by constructional features {, e.g. blending, filament/fibre (piecing of yarns or threads D01H15/00)}
Yarns or threads characterised by the purpose for which they are designed {(protective gloves A41D19/00; protective clothing A62B17/00; ropes D07B1/02)}
